comment.body:
The scriptlets are still needed for AS. Are there any issues with them? 

```adblock
#%#//scriptlet('remove-attr', 'onload|onerror|data', 'head > script[onerror]')
``` 

might cause other issues and can be removed but 

```adblock
#%#//scriptlet('trusted-replace-argument', 'HTMLScriptElement.prototype.setAttribute', '1', 'replace:/.+(error-report|html-load).+//g')
``` 

should be fine for other platforms I think.

labels.name: A: Problematic
labels.description: Issues, which cannot be resolved easily

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://www.dexerto.com/youtube/youtuber-accidentally-crashes-the-rare-plant-market-with-a-viral-cloning-technique-3289808/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.dexerto.com%2Fyoutube%2Fyoutuber-accidentally-crashes-the-rare-plant-market-with-a-viral-cloning-technique-3289808%2F) ### Comment > opening the site flashes an image of a Pokemon team rocket member and then goes to the site that shows it cannot load. ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/rqgx04g8wkgkggc44gc8c4oow0s04gcp1slii3gyy8.png?nc=1)
Screenshot 1 with AdGuard disabled ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/m0fxssb45pss4g0go0cwc04wkog0wk02c83r09w71e.png?nc=1)
### System configuration Information | Value --- | --- AdGuard product: | AdGuard for Mac v2.18.0.2082 nightly Browser: | Safari Tracking protection: | disabled DNS filtering: | disabled Filters: | Ad blocking:
AdGuard Base Browsing Security: | disabled Browsing Security statistics: | disabled Userscripts: | disabled Userstyles: | disabled ### Issue configuration [Import](https://reports.adguard.com/settings/import.html?product_type=mac&product_version=2.18.0.2082+nightly&system_version=&browser=safari&filters=2&dns.filters_enabled=false&dns.enabled=false&browsing_security.enabled=false&browsing_security.statistics_enabled=false&stealth.enabled=false)

comment.body:
Looks like the issue is due to this rule somehow doesn't block `https://html-load.com/loader.min.js` on AG for Android

```adblock
||html-load.com^$script,important,domain=dexerto.com
```

If I just add

```adblock
||html-load.com^$script,important
```

it works, but if I add `domain`, it doesn't work.

The `https://html-load.com/loader.min.js` is added via JS (not in `view-source` so maybe that's why?)

---

I'll re-add the scriptlets since they are not really related to the issue but I don't know how to address the above.

issue.title: advise.co
issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [http://advise.co](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=http%3A%2F%2Fadvise.co) ### Comment > ### Problem description > > `advise.co` appears to be blocked as a false positive. > > When AdGuard is enabled (with default filters), the website loads without styling or functionality. First-party CSS, JavaScript, images, and media served from `advise.co` are blocked, causing the page to render broken. > > The site does not contain ads, tracking, or malicious scripts. All blocked resources are first-party assets. > > ### Steps to reproduce > > 1. Enable AdGuard with default settings (including EasyList). > 2. Open https://advise.co > > ### Expected result > > The website should load normally with all first-party resources allowed. > > ### Actual result > > First-party resources from `advise.co` are blocked, breaking layout and functionality. > > ### Additional information > > `advise.co` is a legitimate B2B SaaS website. > This appears to be a domain-level false positive rather than content-based blocking. ``` Username: @Andrinoid ``` ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/qjvqukt5icggg8ogwgw4kckkgw00gow8vfo7ld66p0.png?nc=1)
Screenshot 1 with AdGuard disabled ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/hk05zpjsznk0oc0sg4sw44kcswcg0swojvpy74juzk.png?nc=1)

issue.title: www.youtube.com

labels.name: T: Anti Adblock Script
labels.description: The site detects ad blocking

labels.name: NSFW
labels.description: Nudes and porn sites

labels.name: A: Problematic
labels.description: Issues, which cannot be resolved easily

issue.body:
# ATTENTION

> [!NOTE]  
> YouTube changes their detection scripts regularly. That means that sometime you can face the detection message.
> If all the following instructions do not work, please **email filters@adguard.com** or wait until any update is available. 

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use another adblocker in the same time because using another adblocker can delay/lags the YouTube loading (e.g., AdBlock Plus and AdBlock)[^1] OR interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use an userscript related to the bypassing YouTube detection message like [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing).
> Recently, YouTube anti-adblock script added infinite dummy advertisements to try to neutralize the userscript. ( [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing#264](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing/issues/264) )

> [!WARNING]
> Please check that there no another web browser extension that has adblocking feature is enabled on your web browser. It can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!IMPORTANT]  
> Saving memory feature of your web browser or sleep feature of your operating system can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.
> In this case, please re-enable AdGuard's filtering by toggling the protection toggle.
> Following the instructions can be needed.

### The instructions
1. Force to update the filters by clicking the reload button in AdGuard Browser Extension/AdGuard mobile or update tray menu in your system tray. [^2]
2. Reload the YouTube webpage without cache by pressing <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd> [^3].
3. Clear cache, cookies and site data of your web browser.
4. Re-open your web browser completely (checking your system monitor[^4] will be better).

[^1]: https://gitlab.com/adblockinc/ext/adblockplus/adblockplusui/-/issues/1576
[^2]: [AdGuard for Windows](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-windows/overview/#tray-menu); [AdGuard for Mac](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-mac/overview/#updates-check); 
[^3]: On macOS, <kbd>Command</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd>
[^4]: Task Manager on Windows, System Monitor on GNOME Shell, KDE System Guard on KDE Plasma or Activity Monitor on macOS. 


Previous issue https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/198973
